findings.title: Readiness accepts invalid or incomplete execution evidence
findings.evidence: scripts/codeops_state.py:123 accepts any non-empty status; :226-240 does not require passing tests, verified tasks, implementation evidence, or passing verification. This conflicts with references/artifacts/traceability.md:5-34 and can produce false READY results.

findings.title: Interrupted migration can leave a false completed marker
findings.evidence: scripts/codeops-migrate.sh:310-321 writes codeops/.codeops.yml before the portfolio at :326, although :289-290 claims the marker is written last. Failure between those writes makes retries exit as already migrated at :75-77.

findings.title: Comparative parity claim exceeds retained evidence
findings.evidence: plans/codex-port/07-testing-strategy.md:83-95 defines a broad comparative suite. scripts/evaluate_scenarios.py:17-42 and compare_scenarios.py:23-40 check token overlap, verdict, and blocker count only. The result schema records no version, commit, timestamp, invocation, or review provenance, yet README.md:7,45 claims passing installed-product parity.

findings.title: Required beta installation and migration evidence is not retained
findings.evidence: The beta gate at plans/codex-port/07-testing-strategy.md:103 requires declared-surface installation/hooks and migration on real-project copies. Retained evidence covers Ubuntu repository checks, synthetic migration fixtures, and limited hook fixtures—not clean lifecycle testing, macOS, live trusted hooks, or real-project migration. README.md:57 nevertheless says installation commands were verified.

findings.title: Governing port state contradicts the beta claim
findings.evidence: plans/codex-port/00-index.md:3 remains at discovery/planning, while plans/codex-port/99-execution-plan.md:3-5 reports Planning, 0/57 with all release tasks unchecked. This contradicts README.md:7 and prevents durable reconstruction of beta completion.

findings.title: Supported platform boundary is omitted
findings.evidence: plans/codex-port/00-ambiguity-register.md:19 limits support to Linux/macOS with Bash and Python 3, but README.md and docs/installation.md do not state this boundary.

findings.title: “Ambiguity-free” overstates deferral semantics
findings.evidence: README.md:3 promises an ambiguity-free plan, while README.md:26 and the governing workflow permit unresolved behavior to be explicitly deferred with recorded risk.

strengths: All five prescribed verification commands pass.
The plugin is currently installed and enabled as 0.2.0-beta.2 in Codex CLI.
Migration, roadmap, and compaction suites include strong hostile-input and idempotency coverage.
The real-complex-project pilot is correctly reserved for 1.0 and was excluded from this verdict.
Independent auditors and challenger converged on all five major findings.
